title: glycine holophylla
intro: Glycine holophylla, also known as the sweet shrub or Missouri snuffbox, is a perennial plant native to North America with a sweet, fragrant bark that has been used for medicinal purposes. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its ability to promote digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural diuretic. The health benefits of Glycine holophylla include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat urinary tract problems and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the bark in hot water or mixing it with a solvent like ethanol.
